findings.title: Disabled failover still performs atomic reset work per URMA result
findings.impact: When operators set urma_failover_success_rate_ratio to 0.0 to disable URMA failover, every Set/Get URMA result still enters RecordUrmaResult and calls ResetWindow, which performs multiple release atomic stores on the foreground data path. On high-QPS clients this makes the documented disabled mode add avoidable cache-line traffic and latency instead of reducing runtime overhead.
findings.fix: Make the disabled fast path cheap: clear tracker state only on the transition to disabled, or guard the call sites so RecordUrmaResult is not invoked while the feature is disabled. Keep the per-request path to a simple read/return when the feature is off.
